vulnerability.title: PromtEngineer localGPT LLM Prompt injection
vulnerability.description: A vulnerability has been found in PromtEngineer localGPT up to 4d41c7d1713b16b216d8e062e51a5dd88b20b054. The impacted element is the function _route_using_overviews of the file backend/server.py of the component LLM Prompt Handler. Such manipulation leads to injection. The attack may be performed from remote. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used. This product utilizes a rolling release system for continuous delivery, and as such, version information for affected or updated releases is not disclosed. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
The vulnerability CVE-2026-5002 is an issue with the way PromtEngineer's local AI model, called LLM Prompt Handler, processes user input, allowing an attacker to inject malicious code.
An attacker exploits this vulnerability by sending a specially crafted request to the system, tricking the application into executing the attacker's code instead of its intended function.
This vulnerability can cause damage by enabling an attacker to seize control over the system, potentially granting access to sensitive data, modifying or deleting important files, and disrupting the normal functioning of the application.

vulnerability.title: Multiple vulnerabilities in 1millionbot Millie chatbot
vulnerability.description: Prompt injection vulnerability in 1millionbot Millie chatbot that occurs when a user manages to evade chat restrictions using Boolean prompt injection techniques (formulating a question in such a way that, upon receiving an affirmative response ('true'), the model executes the injected instruction), causing it to return prohibited information and information outside its intended context. Successful exploitation of this vulnerability could allow a malicious remote attacker to abuse the service for purposes other than those originally intended, or even execute out-of-context tasks using 1millionbot's resources and/or OpenAI's API key. This allows the attacker to evade the containment mechanisms implemented during LLM model training and obtain responses or chat behaviors that were originally restricted.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The vulnerability is a "prompt injection" issue in the 1millionbot Millie chatbot, which allows an attacker to trick the system into providing information that's not supposed to be shared. An attacker exploiting this vulnerability can formulate a question in a way that makes the chatbot answer "true", allowing them to execute unauthorized instructions. Depending on the exploit, this could lead to the attacker accessing sensitive information, using the chatbot's resources for malicious purposes, or even hijacking the OpenAI API key.

vulnerability.title: Flowise: APIChain Prompt Injection SSRF in GET/POST API Chains
vulnerability.description: Flowise is a drag & drop user interface to build a customized large language model flow. Prior to 3.1.0, a Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerability exists in FlowiseAI's POST/GET API Chain components that allows unauthenticated attackers to force the server to make arbitrary HTTP requests to internal and external systems. By injecting malicious prompt templates, attackers can bypass the intended API documentation constraints and redirect requests to sensitive internal services, potentially leading to internal network reconnaissance and data exfiltration. This vulnerability is fixed in 3.1.0.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The vulnerability is a Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) issue in the FlowiseAI's API, allowing attackers to trick the server into making requests to internal or external systems. An attacker exploits this by injecting malicious prompt templates into the API, which can then redirect requests to sensitive internal services. This can cause damage by allowing attackers to gather sensitive information about the internal network and potentially steal data.

vulnerability.title: PraisonAIAgents has SSRF and Local File Read via Unvalidated URLs in web_crawl Tool
vulnerability.description: PraisonAIAgents is a multi-agent teams system. Prior to 1.5.128, the web_crawl() function in praisonaiagents/tools/web_crawl_tools.py accepts arbitrary URLs from AI agents with zero validation. No scheme allowlisting, hostname/IP blocklisting, or private network checks are applied before fetching. This allows an attacker (or prompt injection in crawled content) to force the agent to fetch cloud metadata endpoints, internal services, or local files via file:// URLs. This vulnerability is fixed in 1.5.128.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
Here's a simple explanation of the CVE vulnerability:
The vulnerability is a Prompt Injection flaw in PraisonAIAgents, which allows an attacker to trick the system into making unauthorized HTTP requests to any arbitrary URL, without prior validation. The attacker can exploit this by crafting a malicious prompt or input that tricks the system into requesting sensitive information or local files, such as cloud metadata endpoints or internal services. This can cause significant damage by allowing an attacker to access sensitive data, disrupt system operations, or even gain unauthorized access to internal resources.

vulnerability.title: PraisonAIAgents Affected by Arbitrary File Read via read_skill_file Missing Workspace Boundary and Approval Gate
vulnerability.description: PraisonAIAgents is a multi-agent teams system. Prior to 1.5.128, read_skill_file() in skill_tools.py allows reading arbitrary files from the filesystem by accepting an unrestricted skill_path parameter. Unlike file_tools.read_file which enforces workspace boundary confinement, and unlike run_skill_script which requires critical-level approval, read_skill_file has neither protection. An agent influenced by prompt injection can exfiltrate sensitive files without triggering any approval prompt. This vulnerability is fixed in 1.5.128.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
The vulnerability is a file reading issue in the PraisonAIAgents system, where it allows hackers to read arbitrary files from the computer's file system without needing permission.
An attacker exploits this by using a special kind of text input called a "prompt injection" that tricks the system into reading a different file than the one it was supposed to read. This allows the hacker to steal sensitive files, such as passwords or confidential data, without the system warning or stopping them.
The damage can include the theft of sensitive data, disruption of critical systems or services, or unauthorized access to confidential information, all of which can have serious consequences for the affected organization or individuals.

vulnerability.title: PraisonAIAgents has an OS Command Injection via shell=True in Memory Hooks Executor (memory/)
vulnerability.description: PraisonAIAgents is a multi-agent teams system. Prior to 1.5.128, he memory hooks executor in praisonaiagents passes a user-controlled command string directly to subprocess.run() with shell=True at src/praisonai-agents/praisonaiagents/memory/hooks.py. No sanitization is performed and shell metacharacters are interpreted by /bin/sh before the intended command executes. Two independent attack surfaces exist. The first is via pre_run_command and post_run_command hook event types registered through the hooks configuration. The second and more severe surface is the .praisonai/hooks.json lifecycle configuration, where hooks registered for events such as BEFORE_TOOL and AFTER_TOOL fire automatically during agent operation. An agent that gains file-write access through prompt injection can overwrite .praisonai/hooks.json and have its payload execute silently at every subsequent lifecycle event without further user interaction. This vulnerability is fixed in 1.5.128.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
Here are three simple sentences explaining the vulnerability:
The CVE-2026-40111 vulnerability is a prompt injection vulnerability in the PraisonAIAgents system, which allows attackers to inject malicious commands. An attacker exploits this vulnerability by sending a specially crafted string to the system, which is then executed by the shell, granting the attacker file-write access. With this access, the attacker can overwrite configuration files, allowing malicious code to run automatically without further interaction, potentially leading to system compromise or data theft.

vulnerability.title: MCP Server Kubernetes has Argument Injection in its port_forward tool via space-splitting
vulnerability.description: mcp-server-kubernetes is a Model Context Protocol server for Kubernetes cluster management. Versions 3.4.0 and prior contain an argument injection vulnerability in the port_forward tool in src/tools/port_forward.ts, where a kubectl command is constructed via string concatenation with user-controlled input and then naively split on spaces before being passed to spawn(). Unlike all other tools in the codebase which correctly use array-based argument passing with execFileSync(), port_forward treats every space in user-controlled fields (namespace, resourceType, resourceName, localPort, targetPort) as an argument boundary, allowing an attacker to inject arbitrary kubectl flags. This enables exposure of internal Kubernetes services to the network by injecting --address=0.0.0.0, cross-namespace targeting by injecting additional -n flags, and indirect exploitation via prompt injection against AI agents connected to the MCP server. This issue has been fixed in version 3.5.0.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
Here's an explanation of the CVE vulnerability in 3 simple sentences:
The vulnerability is an Argument Injection issue in the port_forward tool of the mcp-server-kubernetes, which allows an attacker to insert malicious code when a user inputs certain parameters. An attacker exploits this vulnerability by inserting specially crafted commands, known as kubectl flags, that trick the system into exposing internal Kubernetes services or even targeting AI agents. This can cause damage by allowing an attacker to access sensitive system information, launch attacks on network services, or interact with artificial intelligence systems connected to the vulnerable server.

vulnerability.title: Claude Code: Sandbox Escape via Symlink Following Allows Arbitrary File Write Outside Workspace
vulnerability.description: Claude Code is an agentic coding tool. Prior to version 2.1.64, Claude Code's sandbox did not prevent sandboxed processes from creating symlinks pointing to locations outside the workspace. When Claude Code subsequently wrote to a path within such a symlink, its unsandboxed process followed the symlink and wrote to the target location outside the workspace without prompting the user for confirmation. This allowed a sandbox escape where neither the sandboxed command nor the unsandboxed app could independently write outside the workspace, but their combination could write to arbitrary locations, potentially leading to code execution outside the sandbox. Reliably exploiting this required the ability to add untrusted content into a Claude Code context window to trigger sandboxed code execution via prompt injection. Users on standard Claude Code auto-update have received this fix automatically. Users performing manual updates are advised to update to version 2.1.64 or later.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
Here's a simple explanation of the CVE vulnerability:
The vulnerability in Claude Code is a prompt injection issue, which allows an attacker to trick the system into performing unintended actions. An attacker can exploit this vulnerability by adding malicious code into a Claude Code context window, which triggers the system to create a symbolic link that points to a location outside the workspace, allowing the attacker to write to arbitrary locations. This could ultimately lead to code execution outside of the sandbox, potentially giving the attacker full control over the system.

vulnerability.title: OpenClaw 2026.2.13 < 2026.3.25 - ANSI Escape Sequence Injection in Approval Prompt
vulnerability.description: OpenClaw versions 2026.2.13 through 2026.3.24 contain an ANSI escape sequence injection vulnerability in approval prompts that allows attackers to spoof terminal output. Untrusted tool metadata can carry ANSI control sequences into approval prompts and permission logs, enabling attackers to manipulate displayed information through malicious tool titles.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The vulnerability in OpenClaw is called Prompt Injection, which allows an attacker to tamper with the text displayed on a terminal. An attacker can exploit this vulnerability by sending a malicious tool title that contains special ANSI escape sequences, which can be embedded in the terminal output to display incorrect information or make it look like a legitimate prompt. This can trick a user into making a mistake or providing sensitive information, potentially leading to further system compromise or data theft.

vulnerability.title: Anthropic Claude Code & Agent SDK OS Command Injection via promptEditor.ts
vulnerability.description: Anthropic Claude Code CLI and Claude Agent SDK contain an OS command injection vulnerability in the prompt editor invocation utility that allows attackers to execute arbitrary commands by crafting malicious file paths. Attackers can inject shell metacharacters such as $() or backtick expressions into file paths that are interpolated into shell commands executed via execSync. Although the file path is wrapped in double quotes, POSIX shell semantics (POSIX §2.2.3) do not prevent command substitution within double quotes, allowing injected expressions to be evaluated and resulting in arbitrary command execution with the privileges of the user running the CLI.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
The vulnerability in CVE-2026-35021 allows an attacker to inject malicious code, known as OS command injection, which can potentially execute arbitrary system commands.
The attacker exploits this vulnerability by crafting a malicious file path with embedded shell metacharacters, such as dollar signs or backticks, that trick the system into executing a different command than intended.
This vulnerability can cause significant damage by allowing attackers to execute commands with system privileges, potentially leading to data breaches, system crashes, or even complete system takeover, depending on the level of access the attacker gains.

vulnerability.title: Claude SDK for TypeScript: Memory Tool Path Validation Allows Sandbox Escape to Sibling Directories
vulnerability.description: Claude SDK for TypeScript provides access to the Claude API from server-side TypeScript or JavaScript applications. From version 0.79.0 to before version 0.81.0, the local filesystem memory tool in the Anthropic TypeScript SDK validated model-supplied paths using a string prefix check that did not append a trailing path separator. A model steered by prompt injection could supply a crafted path that resolved to a sibling directory sharing the memory root's name as a prefix, allowing reads and writes outside the sandboxed memory directory. This issue has been patched in version 0.81.0.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
Here's an explanation of the CVE vulnerability in 3 simple sentences:
The vulnerability, CVE-2026-34451, is a Prompt Injection flaw in Claude SDK for TypeScript. The attacker exploits it by injecting a malicious prompt that tricks the system into accessing files outside a designated "sandboxed" area, which is meant to prevent unauthorized access. This allows the attacker to read or write sensitive files, potentially leading to data theft, system compromise, or other serious consequences.

vulnerability.title: LangChain Core has Path Traversal vulnerabilites in legacy `load_prompt` functions
vulnerability.description: LangChain is a framework for building agents and LLM-powered applications. Prior to version 1.2.22, multiple functions in langchain_core.prompts.loading read files from paths embedded in deserialized config dicts without validating against directory traversal or absolute path injection. When an application passes user-influenced prompt configurations to load_prompt() or load_prompt_from_config(), an attacker can read arbitrary files on the host filesystem, constrained only by file-extension checks (.txt for templates, .json/.yaml for examples). This issue has been patched in version 1.2.22.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
Here's the explanation of the CVE vulnerability in simple terms:
The vulnerability is a file-reading bug in the LangChain framework that allows an attacker to read any file on the computer where the vulnerable application is running. An attacker can exploit this bug by tricking the application into loading a malicious configuration file that contains the path to the desired file, essentially allowing the attacker to peek into the host computer's files. This vulnerability can cause damage by exposing sensitive information stored on the computer, potentially leading to data breaches or even attacks on the entire system.

vulnerability.title: Azure Data Explorer MCP Server: KQL Injection in multiple tools allows MCP client to execute arbitrary Kusto queries
vulnerability.description: Azure Data Explorer MCP Server is a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that enables AI assistants to execute KQL queries and explore Azure Data Explorer (ADX/Kusto) databases through standardized interfaces. Versions up to and including 0.1.1 contain KQL (Kusto Query Language) injection vulnerabilities in three MCP tool handlers: `get_table_schema`, `sample_table_data`, and `get_table_details`. The `table_name` parameter is interpolated directly into KQL queries via f-strings without any validation or sanitization, allowing an attacker (or a prompt-injected AI agent) to execute arbitrary KQL queries against the Azure Data Explorer cluster. Commit 0abe0ee55279e111281076393e5e966335fffd30 patches the issue.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
Here's a simple explanation of the vulnerability:
The vulnerability is a Kusto Query Language (KQL) injection flaw in the Azure Data Explorer MCP Server, which allows attackers to inject malicious queries into the system. Attackers exploit this flaw by providing a specially crafted "table name" parameter, which injects malicious code into the KQL queries, allowing them to execute arbitrary and potentially damaging commands. This can lead to unauthorized data access, modification, or even deletion, potentially disrupting or stealing sensitive information from the affected Azure Data Explorer cluster.

vulnerability.title: Zero-Click Indirect Prompt Injection and Authentication Bypass via Email Polling
vulnerability.description: nanobot is a personal AI assistant. Prior to version 0.1.6, an indirect prompt injection vulnerability exists in the email channel processing module (`nanobot/channels/email.py`), allowing a remote, unauthenticated attacker to execute arbitrary LLM instructions (and subsequently, system tools) without any interaction from the bot owner. By sending an email containing malicious prompts to the bot's monitored email address, the bot automatically polls, ingests, and processes the email content as highly trusted input, fully bypassing channel isolation and resulting in a stealthy, zero-click attack. Version 0.1.6 patches the issue.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
Here's an explanation of the CVE-2026-33654 vulnerability in 3 simple sentences:
The vulnerability is an indirect prompt injection flaw in the email channel processing module of the nanobot AI assistant, allowing attackers to inject malicious commands into the system. An attacker exploits this vulnerability by sending a specially crafted email to the bot's monitored email address, which the bot then processes as trusted input without any user interaction. This can cause significant damage, including arbitrary system actions, which could lead to data breaches, system crashes, or even complete control of the AI assistant and its connected systems.

vulnerability.title: Cursor Affected by Arbitrary Code Execution via Prompt Injection and Whitelist Bypass
vulnerability.description: Cursor is a code editor built for programming with AI. Prior to 2.0 ,if a visited website contains maliciously crafted instructions, the model may attempt to follow them in order to “assist” the user. When combined with a bypass of the command whitelist mechanism, such indirect prompt injections could result in commands being executed automatically, without the user’s explicit intent, thereby posing a significant security risk. This vulnerability is fixed in 2.0.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
The vulnerability is a "Prompt Injection" flaw in the Cursor code editor that allows malicious commands to be executed on a user's device without their knowledge.
An attacker exploits this by crafting a malicious website that instructs the Cursor AI model to perform certain actions, which can then be used to bypass the security whitelist and execute commands on the user's machine.
This can cause significant damage, including the potential unauthorized access to sensitive data, software installation, or even malicious code execution, all done without the user's awareness or consent.

vulnerability.title: WeKnora: Tool Execution Hijacking via Ambigous Naming Convention In MCP client and Indirect Prompt Injection
vulnerability.description: WeKnora is an LLM-powered framework designed for deep document understanding and semantic retrieval. Prior to version 0.3.0, a vulnerability involving tool name collision and indirect prompt injection allows a malicious remote MCP server to hijack tool execution. By exploiting an ambiguous naming convention in the MCP client (mcp_{service}_{tool}), an attacker can register a malicious tool that overwrites a legitimate one (e.g., tavily_extract). This enables the attacker to redirect LLM execution flow, exfiltrate system prompts, context, and potentially execute other tools with the user's privileges. This issue has been patched in version 0.3.0.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The CVE-2026-30856 vulnerability allows an attacker to hijack the execution of tools in the WeKnora framework by exploiting a naming collision issue. The attacker can do this by registering a malicious tool with a name that is similar to a legitimate one, tricking the system into executing the malicious code instead of the intended tool. This can lead to the attacker being able to redirect the execution flow, steal sensitive information, and potentially execute other tools with the user's privileges.

vulnerability.title: GitHub Copilot CLI allows for dangerous shell expansion patterns that enable arbitrary command execution
vulnerability.description: The shell tool within GitHub Copilot CLI versions prior to and including 0.0.422 can allow arbitrary code execution through crafted bash parameter expansion patterns. An attacker who can influence the commands executed by the agent (e.g., via prompt injection through repository files, MCP server responses, or user instructions) can exploit bash parameter transformation operators to execute hidden commands, bypassing the safety assessment that classifies commands as "read-only." This has been patched in version 0.0.423. The vulnerability stems from how the CLI's shell safety assessment evaluates commands before execution. The safety layer parses and classifies shell commands as either read-only (safe) or write-capable (requires user approval). However, several bash parameter expansion features can embed executable code within arguments to otherwise read-only commands, causing them to appear safe while actually performing arbitrary operations. The specific dangerous patterns are ${var@P}, ${var=value} / ${var:=value}, ${!var}, and nested $(cmd) or <(cmd) inside ${...} expansions. An attacker who can influence command text sent to the shell tool - for example, through prompt injection via malicious repository content (README files, code comments, issue bodies), compromised or malicious MCP server responses, or crafted user instructions containing obfuscated commands - could achieve arbitrary code execution on the user's workstation. This is possible even in permission modes that require user approval for write operations, since the commands can appear to use only read-only utilities to ultimately trigger write operations. Successful exploitation could lead to data exfiltration, file modification, or further system compromise.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The vulnerability, CVE-2026-29783, is a prompt injection flaw in GitHub Copilot CLI's shell tool that allows an attacker to execute arbitrary code by crafting specific bash parameter expansion patterns. The attacker exploits this vulnerability by influencing the commands executed by the agent, which can be done through malicious repository content, compromised server responses, or crafted user instructions. This vulnerability can cause significant damage, such as data exfiltration, file modification, or further system compromise, by allowing an attacker to execute arbitrary code without the user's knowledge or approval.

vulnerability.title: OpenClaw < 2026.2.14 - Arbitrary File Read via Shell Expansion in Safe Bins Allowlist
vulnerability.description: OpenClaw versions prior to 2026.2.14 contain an arbitrary file read vulnerability in the exec-approvals allowlist validation that checks pre-expansion argv tokens but executes using real shell expansion. Attackers with authorization or through prompt-injection attacks can exploit safe binaries like head, tail, or grep with glob patterns or environment variables to disclose files readable by the gateway or node process when host execution is enabled in allowlist mode.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
Here's a simple explanation of the CVE-2026-28463 vulnerability:
The vulnerability is an "arbitrary file read" issue, which means an attacker can access and read any file on the system. An attacker exploits this by using a "prompt injection" attack, where they trick the system into executing malicious code that reads sensitive files. This can cause significant damage, such as the disclosure of sensitive data like passwords, credit card numbers, or confidential documents.

vulnerability.title: OpenClaw < 2026.2.14 - SSRF via Feishu Extension Media Fetching
vulnerability.description: OpenClaw versions prior to 2026.2.14 contain server-side request forgery vulnerabilities in the Feishu extension that allow attackers to fetch attacker-controlled remote URLs without SSRF protections via sendMediaFeishu function and markdown image processing. Attackers can influence tool calls through direct manipulation or prompt injection to trigger requests to internal services and re-upload responses as Feishu media.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The vulnerability, known as CVE-2026-28451, is a type of "Prompt Injection" attack that affects the Feishu extension in OpenClaw versions prior to 2026.2.14. An attacker exploits it by tricking the system into making unauthorized requests to internal services, which can result in the re-upload of manipulated data. This can cause damage by allowing attackers to access sensitive internal data and potentially take control of internal services.

vulnerability.title: Langflow has Remote Code Execution in CSV Agent
vulnerability.description: Langflow is a tool for building and deploying AI-powered agents and workflows. Prior to version 1.8.0, the CSV Agent node in Langflow hardcodes `allow_dangerous_code=True`, which automatically exposes LangChain’s Python REPL tool (`python_repl_ast`). As a result, an attacker can execute arbitrary Python and OS commands on the server via prompt injection, leading to full Remote Code Execution (RCE). Version 1.8.0 fixes the issue.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The CVE-2026-27966 vulnerability is a Prompt Injection attack that allows an attacker to inject malicious code into a system. An attacker exploits this by sending crafted inputs to the LangChain's Python REPL tool, which then executes the attacker's code, allowing them to execute arbitrary Python and OS commands on the server. This can cause full Remote Code Execution (RCE), allowing the attacker to potentially gain complete control over the server.

vulnerability.title: Discourse has Stored XSS in AI Triage Automation
vulnerability.description: Discourse is an open-source discussion platform. Versions prior to 2026.3.0-latest.1, 2026.2.1, and 2026.1.2 have a cross-site scripting vulnerability that arises because the system trusts the raw output from an AI Large Language Model (LLM) and renders it using htmlSafe in the Review Queue interface without adequate sanitization. A malicious attacker can use valid Prompt Injection techniques to force the AI to return a malicious payload (e.g., tags). When a Staff member (Admin/Moderator) views the flagged post in the Review Queue, the payload executes. Versions 2026.3.0-latest.1, 2026.2.1, and 2026.1.2 contain a patch. As a workaround, temporarily disable AI triage automation scripts.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
The vulnerability CVE-2026-27740 is a cross-site scripting (XSS) flaw in the Discourse discussion platform, which allows an attacker to inject malicious code into the system.
An attacker exploits this vulnerability by feeding the AI Large Language Model a specific input, forcing it to return malicious payload, which is then executed when a staff member views a flagged post in the Review Queue.
This exploit can cause damage by allowing an attacker to take control of the system, potentially stealing sensitive information or disrupting the normal operation of the platform.

vulnerability.title: Liquid Prompt arbitrary command injection via crafted Git branch names in gitstatusd backend
vulnerability.description: Liquid Prompt is an adaptive prompt for Bash and Zsh. Starting in commit cf3441250bb5d8b45f6f8b389fcdf427a99ac28a and prior to commit a4f6b8d8c90b3eaa33d13dfd1093062ab9c4b30c on the master branch, arbitrary command injection can lead to code execution when a user enters a directory in a Git repository containing a crafted branch name. Exploitation requires the LP_ENABLE_GITSTATUSD config option to be enabled (enabled by default), gitstatusd to be installed and started before Liquid Prompt is loaded (not the default), and shell prompt substitution to be active (enabled by default in Bash via "shopt -s promptvars", not enabled by default in Zsh). A branch name containing shell syntax such as "$(...)" or backtick expressions in the default branch or a checked-out branch will be evaluated by the shell when the prompt is rendered. No stable release is affected; only the master branch contains the vulnerable commit. Commit a4f6b8d8c90b3eaa33d13dfd1093062ab9c4b30c contains a fix. As a workaround, set the LP_ENABLE_GITSTATUSD config option to 0.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The vulnerability is an issue in the Liquid Prompt tool that can allow attackers to execute arbitrary commands when a user enters a directory in a Git repository with a specially crafted branch name. Attackers exploit this by creating a crafted branch name that includes malicious shell syntax, which is then evaluated by the shell when the prompt is rendered, allowing them to execute their own commands. This exploitation can lead to the attacker gaining access to sensitive information and potentially taking control of the targeted system.

vulnerability.title: OpenClaw has a local file disclosure via sendMediaFeishu in Feishu extension
vulnerability.description: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant. Prior to OpenClaw version 2026.2.14, the Feishu extension previously allowed `sendMediaFeishu` to treat attacker-controlled `mediaUrl` values as local filesystem paths and read them directly. If an attacker can influence tool calls (directly or via prompt injection), they may be able to exfiltrate local files by supplying paths such as `/etc/passwd` as `mediaUrl`. Upgrade to OpenClaw `2026.2.14` or newer to receive a fix. The fix removes direct local file reads from this path and routes media loading through hardened helpers that enforce local-root restrictions.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The vulnerability, known as CVE-2026-26321, is a Prompt Injection flaw in the OpenClaw personal AI assistant, which allows an attacker to manipulate user interactions to execute malicious actions. An attacker exploits this vulnerability by injecting malicious input, such as a request to access a sensitive file, and tricking the AI assistant into reading it directly from the local filesystem. This can cause significant damage by allowing an attacker to exfiltrate sensitive local files, potentially compromising sensitive information and putting the affected system at risk.

vulnerability.title: Cursor sandbox escape via Git hooks
vulnerability.description: Cursor is a code editor built for programming with AI. Sandbox escape via writing .git configuration was possible in versions prior to 2.5. A malicious agent (ie prompt injection) could write to improperly protected .git settings, including git hooks, which may cause out-of-sandbox RCE next time they are triggered. No user interaction was required as Git executes these commands automatically. Fixed in version 2.5.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The vulnerability is a "Prompt Injection" flaw in the Cursor code editor, which allows an attacker to manipulate settings without user interaction. An attacker can exploit this by writing malicious code to the .git configuration, allowing them to execute commands remotely when the Git settings are triggered automatically. This can lead to a complete takeover of the system and execution of arbitrary code, causing significant damage and potentially allowing the attacker to steal sensitive information.

vulnerability.title: Skill Scanner Unsecured Network Binding Vulnerability
vulnerability.description: Skill Scanner is a security scanner for AI Agent Skills that detects prompt injection, data exfiltration, and malicious code patterns. A vulnerability in the API Server of Skill Scanner could allow a unauthenticated, remote attacker to interact with the server API and either trigger a denial of service (DoS) condition or upload arbitrary files. This vulnerability is due to an erroneous binding to multiple interfaces. An attacker could exploit this vulnerability by sending API requests to a device exposing the affected API Server. A successful exploit could allow the attacker to consume an excessive amount of resources (memory starvation) or to upload files to arbitrary folders on the affected device. This vulnerability affects Skill-scanner 1.0.1 and earlier releases when the API Server is enabled. The API Server is not enabled by default. Skill-scanner software releases 1.0.2 and later contain the fix for this vulnerability.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
Here's a simple explanation of the vulnerability:
The CVE-2026-26057 vulnerability is a security weakness in the API Server of Skill Scanner that allows an unauthenticated attacker to interact with the server and cause harm. An attacker exploits this weakness by sending malicious requests to the API Server, which can lead to either a denial-of-service (DoS) condition or the ability to upload arbitrary files to the affected device. This vulnerability can cause significant damage by allowing an attacker to consume excessive resources or upload malicious files to arbitrary folders, potentially compromising the device's security and integrity.

vulnerability.title: Claude Code has a Command Injection in find Command Bypasses User Approval Prompt
vulnerability.description: Claude Code is an agentic coding tool. Prior to version 2.0.72, due to an error in command parsing, it was possible to bypass the Claude Code confirmation prompt to trigger execution of untrusted commands through the find command. Reliably exploiting this required the ability to add untrusted content into a Claude Code context window. This issue has been patched in version 2.0.72.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
The vulnerability is a Prompt Injection bug in Claude Code, an agentic coding tool, which allows malicious input to bypass its confirmation prompt.
An attacker exploits this bug by adding malicious content to a Claude Code context window, tricking it into executing untrusted commands. This can lead to unauthorized code execution or data modification.
The damage caused by this vulnerability can include unintended changes to sensitive data, unauthorized access to system resources, or even system compromise if exploited by a skilled attacker.

vulnerability.title: OpenClaw has Remote Code Execution via System Prompt Injection in Slack Channel Descriptions
vulnerability.description: OpenClaw (formerly Clawdbot) is a personal AI assistant users run on their own devices. In versions 2026.2.2 and below, when the Slack integration is enabled, channel metadata (topic/description) can be incorporated into the model's system prompt. Prompt injection is a documented risk for LLM-driven systems. This issue increases the injection surface by allowing untrusted Slack channel metadata to be treated as higher-trust system input. This issue has been fixed in version 2026.2.3.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The vulnerability in OpenClaw is called a prompt injection flaw, allowing hackers to influence the AI assistant to execute unauthorized system actions. Attackers can exploit this flaw by sending malicious channel metadata to OpenClaw's Slack integration, tricking the AI into performing actions it's not supposed to, such as accessing sensitive user data or executing system commands. This vulnerability can cause significant damage, including data breaches, unauthorized access, and disruption of system functions, giving hackers control over the user's AI assistant.

vulnerability.title: MySQLSelectTool Read-Only Bypass via SELECT INTO OUTFILE Allows Arbitrary File Write
vulnerability.description: Neuron is a PHP framework for creating and orchestrating AI Agents. Versions 2.8.11 and below use MySQLSelectTool, which is vulnerable to Read-Only Bypass. MySQLSelectTool is intended to be a read-only SQL tool (e.g., for LLM agent querying, however, validation based on the first keyword (e.g., SELECT) and a forbidden-keyword list does not block file-writing constructs such as INTO OUTFILE / INTO DUMPFILE. As a result, an attacker who can influence the tool input (e.g., via prompt injection through a public agent endpoint) may write arbitrary files to the DB server if the MySQL/MariaDB account has the FILE privilege and server configuration permits writes to a useful location (e.g., a web-accessible directory). This issue is fixed in version 2.8.12.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The vulnerability, identified as CVE-2025-67509, is a weakness in the MySQLSelectTool that allows an attacker to bypass read-only restrictions and write files to a database server. An attacker can exploit this vulnerability by injecting specific prompts into a public agent endpoint, which can then be used to write arbitrary files on the database server, potentially allowing the attacker to steal sensitive data or disrupt the system. The impact of this vulnerability could result in unauthorized data access or manipulation, as well as potentially allowing an attacker to gain control over the database server.

vulnerability.title: LangChain Vulnerable to Template Injection via Attribute Access in Prompt Templates
vulnerability.description: LangChain is a framework for building agents and LLM-powered applications. From versions 0.3.79 and prior and 1.0.0 to 1.0.6, a template injection vulnerability exists in LangChain's prompt template system that allows attackers to access Python object internals through template syntax. This vulnerability affects applications that accept untrusted template strings (not just template variables) in ChatPromptTemplate and related prompt template classes. This issue has been patched in versions 0.3.80 and 1.0.7.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
The CVE-2025-65106 vulnerability is a type of injection issue, called prompt injection, that allows attackers to manipulate the internal workings of a LangChain framework by inserting malicious template strings.
An attacker can exploit this vulnerability by sending a specially crafted template string to an application that uses the LangChain framework, which can allow the attacker to access and manipulate the application's internal data.
If successfully exploited, the vulnerability can lead to unauthorized access to an application's sensitive data, potentially causing data breaches, corruption, and other serious security risks.

vulnerability.title: Cursor: Authentication Bypass Possible via New Cursorignore Write
vulnerability.description: Cursor is a code editor built for programming with AI. In versions 1.7.23 and below, a logic bug allows a malicious agent to read sensitive files that should be protected via cursorignore. An attacker who has already achieved prompt injection, or a malicious model, could create a new cursorignore file which can invalidate the configuration of pre-existing ones. This could allow a malicious agent to read protected files. This issue is fixed in version 2.0.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
Here's a simple explanation of the vulnerability:
The vulnerability is a logic bug in the Cursor code editor that allows a malicious agent to read sensitive files protected by the cursorignore configuration. An attacker can exploit this by creating a new file that invalidates the existing cursorignore configuration, allowing them to access and read protected files. This can cause significant damage by giving attackers unrestricted access to sensitive data, potentially leading to data theft, intellectual property loss, or even financial losses.

vulnerability.title: Cursor's Sensitive File Modification can Lead to NTFS Path Quirks
vulnerability.description: Cursor is a code editor built for programming with AI. In versions 1.7.44 and below, various NTFS path quirks allow a prompt injection attacker to circumvent sensitive file protections and overwrite files which Cursor requires human approval to overwrite. Modification of some of the protected files can lead to RCE. Must be chained with a prompt injection or malicious model attach. Only affects systems supporting NTFS. This issue is fixed in version 2.0.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
Here's a simple explanation of the vulnerability:
The vulnerability, CVE-2025-64108, is a flaw in Cursor's code editor that allows attackers to bypass file protection. An attacker exploits this by tricking Cursor into modifying sensitive files without proper approval, which could allow them to take full control of the system. This can cause remote code execution (RCE), giving the attacker the power to do anything they want with the compromised system.

vulnerability.title: Cursor is Vulnerable to Path Manipulation Using Backslashes on Windows
vulnerability.description: Cursor is a code editor built for programming with AI. In versions 1.7.52 and below, manipulating internal settings may lead to RCE. Cursor detects path manipulation via forward slashes (./.cursor/./././././mcp.json etc.), and requires human approval to complete the operation. However, the same kind of manipulation using backslashes was not correctly detected, allowing an attacker who had already achieved prompt injection or some other level of control to overwrite sensitive editor files without approval on Windows machines. This issue is fixed in version 2.0.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
Here are 3 simple sentences explaining the vulnerability:
The vulnerability is a Remote Code Execution (RCE) flaw in the Cursor code editor, specifically affecting versions 1.7.52 and below, allowing an attacker to gain control of the system. An attacker exploits this by manipulating file paths using backslashes, which the Cursor system mistakenly believes requires user approval, allowing the attacker to overwrite sensitive files without being detected. This can cause significant damage by granting an attacker full control over the system, essentially allowing them to execute arbitrary code and potentially access sensitive information or spread malware.

vulnerability.title: Cursor CLI Agent: Sensitive File Overwrite Bypass
vulnerability.description: Cursor is a code editor built for programming with AI. In versions 1.7 and below, a vulnerability in the way Cursor CLI Agent protects its sensitive files (i.e. */.cursor/cli.json) allows attackers to modify the content of the files through prompt injection, thus achieving remote code execution. A prompt injection can lead to full RCE through modifying sensitive files on case-insensitive filesystems. This issue is fixed in a commit, 25b418f, but has yet to be released as of October 3, 2025.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
Here are three simple sentences explaining the vulnerability:
The vulnerability is a Prompt Injection flaw in Cursor's CLI Agent, which allows an attacker to manipulate sensitive files on the system. An attacker exploits it by tricking the system into executing a malicious command, which modifies the CLI Agent's files, and gains full control of the system. The damage this can cause is remote code execution (RCE), allowing an attacker to access, modify, or delete sensitive data on the system.

vulnerability.title: Cursor CLI: Arbitrary Code Execution Possible through Permissive CLI Config
vulnerability.description: Cursor is a code editor built for programming with AI. In versions 1.7 and below, automatic loading of project-specific CLI configuration from the current working directory (<project>/.cursor/cli.json) could override certain global configurations in Cursor CLI. This allowed users running the CLI inside a malicious repository to be vulnerable to Remote Code Execution through a combination of permissive configuration (allowing shell commands) and prompt injection delivered via project-specific Rules (<project>/.cursor/rules/rule.mdc) or other mechanisms. The fix for this issue is currently available as a patch 2025.09.17-25b418f. As of October 3, 2025 there is no release version.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
The vulnerability in Cursor CLI is a configuration override issue, where a user's settings can be overridden by a malicious project-specific configuration file.
An attacker can exploit this vulnerability by creating a malicious repository that includes a specially crafted configuration file, allowing the attacker to inject malicious commands and execute them on the victim's system.
This vulnerability can cause Remote Code Execution, allowing an attacker to take control of the victim's system and potentially steal sensitive information or install malicious software.

vulnerability.title: Cursor is vulnerable to RCE via .code-workspace files using Prompt Injection
vulnerability.description: Cursor is a code editor built for programming with AI. Versions 1.6 and below are vulnerable to Remote Code Execution (RCE) attacks through Visual Studio Code Workspaces. Workspaces allow users to open more than a single folder and save specific settings (pretty similar to .vscode/settings.json) for the folders / project. An untitled workspace is automatically created by VS Code (untitled.code-workspace), which contains all the folders and workspace settings from the user's current session, opening up an entire new attack vector if the user has a .code-workspace file in path (either untitled created automatically or a saved one). If an attacker is able to hijack the chat context of the victim (such as via a compromised MCP server), they can use prompt injection to make the Cursor Agent write into this file and modify the workspace. This leads to a bypass of CVE-2025-54130 which can lead to RCE by writing to the settings section. This issue is fixed in version 1.7.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
The vulnerability is a code injection flaw in the Cursor code editor that allows attackers to execute malicious code remotely.
An attacker can hijack a user's chat context, typically through a compromised chat server, and inject malicious code into the Cursor Agent. This code is then executed by the Agent, which modifies the user's workspace settings.
The damage this vulnerability can cause includes full control of the victim's machine, as the attacker gains the ability to inject and execute arbitrary code, leading to potentially catastrophic consequences.

vulnerability.title: Cursor: Potential Information Leakage via Mermaid Diagram
vulnerability.description: Cursor is a code editor built for programming with AI. In versions 1.6 and below, Mermaid (a to render diagrams) allows embedding images which then get rendered by Cursor in the chat box. An attacker can use this to exfiltrate sensitive information to a third-party attacker controlled server through an image fetch after successfully performing a prompt injection. A malicious model (or hallucination/backdoor) might also trigger this exploit at will. This issue requires prompt injection from malicious data (web, image upload, source code) in order to exploit. In that case, it can send sensitive information to an attacker-controlled external server. Some additional bypasses not covered in the initial fix to this issue were discovered, see GHSA-43wj-mwcc-x93p. This issue is fixed in version 1.7.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
Here's a simple explanation of the CVE-2025-61589 vulnerability:
The vulnerability is called prompt injection in Mermaid, a tool used by the Cursor code editor to render diagrams. An attacker exploits this by tricking the editor into sending sensitive information to a third-party server, allowing the attacker to extract confidential data. If left unpatched, this vulnerability can cause sensitive data to be stolen and potentially leaked, compromising user privacy and security.

vulnerability.title: Cursor IDE: Sensitive File Overwrite Bypass is Possible
vulnerability.description: Cursor is a code editor built for programming with AI. Versions 1.6.23 and below contain case-sensitive checks in the way Cursor IDE protects its sensitive files (e.g., */.cursor/mcp.json), which allows attackers to modify the content of these files through prompt injection and achieve remote code execution. A prompt injection can lead to full RCE through modifying sensitive files on case-insensitive fileystems. This issue is fixed in version 1.7.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The CVE-2025-59944 vulnerability is a case of sensitive file protection bypass, allowing attackers to modify sensitive files in the Cursor IDE code editor. An attacker exploits this vulnerability through "prompt injection," where they use specific inputs to trick the editor into changing sensitive files, ultimately giving them control over the system. This can lead to remote code execution, allowing the attacker to take control of the computer and perform malicious actions.

vulnerability.title: TCC Bypass via Dylib Injection in Postbox
vulnerability.description: The Postbox's configuration on macOS, specifically the presence of entitlements: "com.apple.security.cs.allow-dyld-environment-variables" and "com.apple.security.cs.disable-library-validation" allows for Dynamic Library (Dylib) injection. A local attacker with unprivileged access can use environment variables like DYLD_INSERT_LIBRARIES to successfully inject code in application's context and bypass Transparency, Consent, and Control (TCC). Acquired resource access is limited to previously granted permissions by the user. Access to other resources beyond granted-permissions requires user interaction with a system prompt asking for permission. The original company behind Postbox is no longer operational, the software will no longer receive updates. The acquiring company (em Client) did not cooperate in vulnerability disclosure.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The vulnerability, CVE-2025-5963, is a security weakness in Postbox's configuration on macOS that allows an attacker to inject malicious code into the application, effectively bypassing security restrictions. An attacker with local, unprivileged access can exploit this vulnerability by using environment variables to inject code, which can prompt the user for additional permissions, potentially leading to unauthorized access to system resources. This could result in damage to the system, potentially allowing the attacker to steal sensitive data, install malware, or disrupt system functionality, and since Postbox won't receive updates, this hole will likely be permanent.

vulnerability.title: Lobe Chat Desktop Vulnerable to Remote Code Execution via XSS in Chat Messages
vulnerability.description: Lobe Chat is an open-source artificial intelligence chat framework. Prior to version 1.129.4, there is a a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability when handling chat message in lobe-chat that can be escalated to remote code execution on the user’s machine. In lobe-chat, when the response from the server is like <lobeArtifact identifier="ai-new-interpretation" ...> , it will be rendered with the lobeArtifact node, instead of the plain text. However, when the type of the lobeArtifact is image/svg+xml , it will be rendered as the SVGRender component, which internally uses dangerouslySetInnerHTML to set the content of the svg, resulting in XSS attack. Any party capable of injecting content into chat messages, such as hosting a malicious page for prompt injection, operating a compromised MCP server, or leveraging tool integrations, can exploit this vulnerability. This vulnerability is fixed in 1.129.4.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
Here's an explanation of the CVE-2025-59417 vulnerability in 3 simple sentences:
The vulnerability is a cross-site scripting (XSS) issue in the Lobe Chat framework, which allows attackers to inject malicious code into the chat messages.
Attackers exploit this by creating a fake webpage that tricks users into sending malicious chat messages, which are then executed on the user's machine as if they were legitimate code. This can cause damage by allowing attackers to steal sensitive information, take control of the user's machine, or even install malware.

vulnerability.title: Claude Code rg command had Command Injection that allowed bypass of user approval prompt for command execution
vulnerability.description: Claude Code is an agentic coding tool. Due to an error in command parsing, versions prior to 1.0.105 were vulnerable to a bypass of the Claude Code confirmation prompt to trigger execution of an untrusted command. Reliably exploiting this requires the ability to add untrusted content into a Claude Code context window. Users on standard Claude Code auto-update will have received this fix automatically. Users performing manual updates are advised to update to version 1.0.105 or the latest version.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The vulnerability, CVE-2025-58764, is a type of attack called Prompt Injection, where an attacker tricks the system into executing commands without warning the user. An attacker exploits this by inserting malicious content into a Claude Code context window, which the system mistakenly interprets as a legitimate command. This allows the attacker to run unauthorized code, potentially leading to data theft, system crashes, or even full system takeover.

vulnerability.title: Roo Code: Potential Remote Code Execution via .code-workspace
vulnerability.description: Roo Code is an AI-powered autonomous coding agent that lives in users' editors. Versions 3.25.23 and below contain a vulnerability where certain VS Code workspace configuration files (.code-workspace) are not protected in the same way as the .vscode folder. If the agent was configured to auto-approve file writes, an attacker able to influence prompts (for example via prompt injection) could cause malicious workspace settings or tasks to be written. These tasks could then be executed automatically when the workspace is reopened, resulting in arbitrary code execution. This issue is fixed in version 3.26.0.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The CVE-2025-58372 vulnerability is a prompt injection vulnerability in Roo Code, an AI-powered coding agent, that allows attackers to execute malicious tasks. An attacker exploits this by influencing the prompts that Roo Code uses, which can cause the agent to write malicious workspace settings or tasks, and these tasks can then be executed automatically when the workspace is reopened. This can cause the attacker to run arbitrary code, potentially allowing them to access or modify data, or even take control of the system.

vulnerability.title: 5ire Chat Message XSS Vulnerability Enables Remote Code Execution
vulnerability.description: 5ire is a cross-platform desktop artificial intelligence assistant and model context protocol client. Version 0.13.2 contains a vulnerability in the chat page's script gadgets that enables content injection attacks through multiple vectors: malicious prompt injection pages, compromised MCP servers, and exploited tool integrations. This is fixed in version 0.14.0.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
Here's a simple explanation of the CVE-2025-58357 vulnerability:
The vulnerability, known as Prompt Injection, allows an attacker to inject malicious code into a 5ire chat page through various means. By exploiting this vulnerability, an attacker can gain unauthorized access to a user's account or sensitive information, which can be used for malicious purposes such as stealing personal data or spreading malware. The damage caused by this vulnerability can include the theft of sensitive information, unauthorized changes to user accounts, and potentially even complete control of the user's system or device.

vulnerability.title: Cursor Agent is vulnerable to prompt injection via MCP Special Files
vulnerability.description: Cursor is a code editor built for programming with AI. Cursor allows writing in-workspace files with no user approval in versions below 1.3.9, If the file is a dotfile, editing it requires approval but creating a new one doesn't. Hence, if sensitive MCP files, such as the .cursor/mcp.json file don't already exist in the workspace, an attacker can chain a indirect prompt injection vulnerability to hijack the context to write to the settings file and trigger RCE on the victim without user approval. This is fixed in version 1.3.9.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The vulnerability, known as CVE-2025-54135, is a prompt injection flaw in the Cursor code editor that allows an attacker to secretly modify sensitive settings files. The attacker exploits this by creating a new file that allows them to inject malicious code into the settings, giving them unauthorized access to the system; in this case, allowing Remote Code Execution (RCE). This vulnerability can cause significant damage, as it enables an attacker to gain remote access to a system and potentially steal sensitive data, disrupt operations, or install malware.

vulnerability.title: Cursor's Mermaid Diagram Tool is Vulnerable to an Arbitrary Image Fetch
vulnerability.description: Cursor is a code editor built for programming with AI. In versions below 1.3, Mermaid (which is used to render diagrams) allows embedding images which then get rendered by Cursor in the chat box. An attacker can use this to exfiltrate sensitive information to a third-party attacker controlled server through an image fetch after successfully performing a prompt injection. A malicious model (or hallucination/backdoor) might also trigger this exploit at will. This issue requires prompt injection from malicious data (web, image upload, source code) in order to exploit. In that case, it can send sensitive information to an attacker-controlled external server. This is fixed in version 1.3.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
The vulnerability, known as CVE-2025-54132, is a prompt injection flaw in the Cursor code editor that allows an attacker to inject malicious data into the system.
An attacker exploits this vulnerability by uploading malicious data to the system, such as a malicious image or code, which contains a command that instructs the system to fetch sensitive information and send it to an attacker-controlled server. This can be done through various means, including web uploads or malicious source code.
This vulnerability can cause significant damage by allowing an attacker to exfiltrate sensitive information, such as confidential data or user credentials, to an external server controlled by the attacker, potentially leading to data theft or identity compromise.

vulnerability.title: Cursor bypasses its allow list to execute arbitrary commands
vulnerability.description: Cursor is a code editor built for programming with AI. In versions below 1.3, an attacker can bypass the allow list in auto-run mode with a backtick (`) or $(cmd). If a user has swapped Cursor from its default settings (requiring approval for every terminal call) to an allowlist, an attacker can execute arbitrary command execution outside of the allowlist without user approval. An attacker can trigger this vulnerability if chained with indirect prompt injection. This is fixed in version 1.3.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The vulnerability is a "Prompt Injection" issue in Cursor, a code editor, where an attacker can bypass the approved list of commands when using the auto-run mode. An attacker exploits this by inserting special characters, like a backtick or dollar sign, into a prompt, allowing them to execute arbitrary commands without user approval. This can cause significant damage, as it enables an attacker to execute unauthorized commands, potentially leading to data theft or system compromise.

vulnerability.title: Cursor Agent is vulnerable prompt injection via Editor Special Files
vulnerability.description: Cursor is a code editor built for programming with AI. Cursor allows writing in-workspace files with no user approval in versions less than 1.3.9. If the file is a dotfile, editing it requires approval but creating a new one doesn't. Hence, if sensitive editor files, such as the .vscode/settings.json file don't already exist in the workspace, an attacker can chain a indirect prompt injection vulnerability to hijack the context to write to the settings file and trigger RCE on the victim without user approval. This is fixed in version 1.3.9.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
The vulnerability is a Prompt Injection flaw in the Cursor code editor, which allows an attacker to create sensitive files without user consent.
An attacker exploits this by using a dotfile (a hidden file) to inject malicious code into the editor's settings file, allowing them to gain remote code execution (RCE) on the victim's system without needing their approval.
The damage caused by this vulnerability can lead to a full remote code execution (RCE) on the victim's system, giving the attacker complete control over the affected device.

vulnerability.title: GitHub Copilot and Visual Studio Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
vulnerability.description: Improper neutralization of special elements used in a command ('command injection') in GitHub Copilot and Visual Studio allows an unauthorized attacker to execute code locally.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
Here's a 3-sentence explanation of the CVE vulnerability:
The vulnerability is a code injection bug in GitHub Copilot and Visual Studio that allows an attacker to input malicious code and execute it on a user's computer without their permission. An attacker exploits this bug by sending manipulated code in a prompt, tricking the system into running it locally, which can lead to data loss, corruption, or even complete system takeover. This vulnerability can cause significant damage by giving an attacker the ability to access and manipulate sensitive information, install malware, or disrupt critical services.

vulnerability.title: @cyanheads/git-mcp-server vulnerable to command injection in several tools
vulnerability.description: @cyanheads/git-mcp-server is an MCP server designed to interact with Git repositories. Prior to version 2.1.5, there is a command injection vulnerability caused by the unsanitized use of input parameters within a call to child_process.exec, enabling an attacker to inject arbitrary system commands. Successful exploitation can lead to remote code execution under the server process's privileges. The server constructs and executes shell commands using unvalidated user input directly within command-line strings. This introduces the possibility of shell metacharacter injection (|, >, &&, etc.). An MCP Client can be instructed to execute additional actions for example via indirect prompt injection when asked to read git logs. This issue has been patched in version 2.1.5.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The vulnerability, known as CVE-2025-53107, occurs when an attacker can inject malicious commands into a system through user input, allowing them to take control of the server process. The attacker exploits this vulnerability by sending specially crafted input to the server, which then unknowingly executes the attacker's malicious commands, giving the attacker the ability to control the server and access sensitive data. If left unpatched, this vulnerability can lead to severe damage, including the theft of sensitive data, disruption of services, and potentially even system compromise.

vulnerability.title: Roo Code extension vulnerable to Potential Information Leakage via JSON Schema
vulnerability.description: Roo Code is an AI-powered autonomous coding agent. Prior to version 3.20.3, there was an issue where the Roo Code agent's `search_files` tool did not respect the setting to disable reads outside of the VS Code workspace. This means that an attacker who was able to inject a prompt into the agent could potentially read a sensitive file and then write the information to a JSON schema. Users have the option to disable schema fetching in VS Code, but the feature is enabled by default. For users with this feature enabled, writing to the schema would trigger a network request without the user having a chance to deny. This issue is of moderate severity, since it requires the attacker to already be able to submit prompts to the agent. Version 3.20.3 fixed the issue where `search_files` did not respect the setting to limit it to the workspace. This reduces the scope of the damage if an attacker is able to take control of the agent through prompt injection or another vector.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
Here's a simple explanation of the CVE vulnerability:
The vulnerability is a Prompt Injection issue in the Roo Code AI-powered coding agent, which allows an attacker to inject a prompt that forces the agent to read a sensitive file.
An attacker exploits this vulnerability by submitting a malicious prompt to the Roo Code agent, which tricks it into reading and extracting sensitive information from outside the designated workspace.
This allows the attacker to gain access to sensitive data and potentially use it to their advantage, such as breaching confidentiality or exploiting the information for malicious purposes.

vulnerability.title: Command Injection in MCP Server ios-simulator-mcp
vulnerability.description: iOS Simulator MCP Server (ios-simulator-mcp) is a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for interacting with iOS simulators. Versions prior to 1.3.3 are written in a way that is vulnerable to command injection vulnerability attacks as part of some of its MCP Server tool definition and implementation. The MCP Server exposes the tool `ui_tap` which relies on Node.js child process API `exec` which is an unsafe and vulnerable API if concatenated with untrusted user input. LLM exposed user input for `duration`, `udid`, and `x` and `y` args can be replaced with shell meta-characters like `;` or `&&` or others to change the behavior from running the expected command `idb` to another command. When LLMs are tricked through prompt injection (and other techniques and attack vectors) to call the tool with input that uses special shell characters such as `; rm -rf /tmp;#` and other payload variations, the full command-line text will be interepted by the shell and result in other commands except of `ps` executing on the host running the MCP Server. Version 1.3.3 contains a patch for the issue.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
Here are 3 simple sentences explaining the CVE vulnerability:
The vulnerability, known as command injection, occurs when an attacker can trick the iOS Simulator's MCP Server into executing unintended commands. The attacker can do this by sending specially crafted input that contains shell meta-characters, such as semicolons or ampersands, which can modify the command being executed. This can allow the attacker to execute arbitrary commands on the host system, potentially leading to data theft, system compromise, or other severe damage.

vulnerability.title: TCC Bypass via Dylib Injection in Phoenix Code
vulnerability.description: The Phoenix Code's configuration on macOS, specifically the presence of entitlements: "com.apple.security.cs.allow-dyld-environment-variables" and "com.apple.security.cs.disable-library-validation" allows for Dynamic Library (Dylib) injection. A local attacker with unprivileged access can use environment variables like DYLD_INSERT_LIBRARIES to successfully inject code in application's context and bypass Transparency, Consent, and Control (TCC). Acquired resource access is limited to previously granted permissions by the user. Access to other resources beyond granted-permissions requires user interaction with a system prompt asking for permission. This issue was fixed in commit 0c75fb57f89d0b7d9b180026bc2624b7dcf807da
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The vulnerability CVE-2025-5255 is a code injection bug that allows an attacker to inject malicious code into a legitimate app on a macOS system. The attacker exploits this by setting a specific environment variable, such as DYLD_INSERT_LIBRARIES, to run their own code alongside the regular app code, essentially hacking into the app. This can cause damage by allowing the attacker to access sensitive data or perform unauthorized actions on the system, which could lead to data theft or system compromise.

vulnerability.title: Cursor Agent Potentially Leaks Information using JSON schema
vulnerability.description: Cursor is a code editor built for programming with AI. Prior to 0.51.0, by default, the setting json.schemaDownload.enable was set to True. This means that by writing a JSON file, an attacker can trigger an arbitrary HTTP GET request that does not require user confirmation. Since the Cursor Agent can edit JSON files, this means a malicious agent, for example, after a prompt injection attack already succeeded, could trigger a GET request to an attacker controlled URL, potentially exfiltrating other data the agent may have access to. This vulnerability is fixed in 0.51.0.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The vulnerability, CVE-2025-49150, is a prompt injection flaw in the Cursor code editor, which allows an attacker to manipulate the editor into making unintended HTTP requests. An attacker exploits this vulnerability by writing a malicious JSON file, which tricks the editor into sending sensitive information to a controlled URL without user confirmation. If successful, the attack can lead to data exfiltration, allowing the attacker to access and steal other sensitive information that the affected agent may have access to.

vulnerability.title: Terraform WinDNS Provider improperly sanitizes input variables in `windns_record`
vulnerability.description: Terraform WinDNS Provider allows users to manage their Windows DNS server resources through Terraform. A security issue has been found in Terraform WinDNS Provider before version `1.0.5`. The `windns_record` resource did not sanitize the input variables. This could lead to authenticated command injection in the underlyding powershell command prompt. Version 1.0.5 contains a fix for the issue.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
The vulnerability, known as CVE-2025-46735, involves a problem with the Terraform WinDNS provider where it does not properly sanitize user input, potentially allowing malicious commands to be executed.
An attacker exploits this vulnerability by injecting malicious commands into the Terraform configuration, which are then executed by the Windows PowerShell command prompt. This allows the attacker to gain unauthorized access or control over the affected system.
The damage caused by this vulnerability can lead to unauthorized access, data tampering, or even full system compromise, as malicious commands can be executed with the same privileges as the Terraform user account.

vulnerability.title: YoutubeDLSharp allows command injection on windows system due to non sanitized arguments
vulnerability.description: YoutubeDLSharp is a wrapper for the command-line video downloaders youtube-dl and yt-dlp. In versions starting from 1.0.0-beta4 and prior to 1.1.2, an unsafe conversion of arguments allows the injection of a malicious commands when starting `yt-dlp` from a commands prompt running on Windows OS with the `UseWindowsEncodingWorkaround` value defined to true (default behavior). If a user is using built-in methods from the YoutubeDL.cs file, the value is true by default and a user cannot disable it from these methods. This issue has been patched in version 1.1.2.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The vulnerability is a "Prompt Injection" issue in the YoutubeDLSharp library, which allows an attacker to inject malicious commands into the system. An attacker exploits this vulnerability by using crafted input to execute unauthorized commands when a user runs the 'yt-dlp' command, allowing the attacker to potentially gain control of the system. This can cause damage by allowing the attacker to execute arbitrary system commands, which can lead to system compromise, data theft, or other malicious activities.

vulnerability.title: Code Injection Vulnerability in AiDex
vulnerability.description: In versions prior to Aidex 1.7, an authenticated malicious user, taking advantage of an open registry, could execute unauthorised commands within the system. This includes executing operating system (Unix) commands, interacting with internal services such as PHP or MySQL, and even invoking native functions of the framework used, such as Laravel or Symfony. This execution is achieved by Prompt Injection attacks through the /api/<string-chat>/message endpoint, manipulating the content of the ‘content’ parameter.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
Here are the explanations in 3 simple sentences:
The vulnerability, known as CVE-2025-3579, is a Prompt Injection issue in Aidex versions prior to 1.7 that allows an authenticated malicious user to execute arbitrary system commands.
An attacker exploits this vulnerability by manipulating the 'content' parameter in the /api/<string-chat>/message endpoint, forcing Aidex to run unwanted commands, which can interact with the system, invoke internal services, or access native functions within the framework.
This vulnerability can cause significant damage, allowing an attacker to potentially take control of the system, steal sensitive data, or perform other malicious actions that compromise the security and integrity of the Aidex system and the surrounding environment.

vulnerability.title: Arbitrary file write from Cursor Agent through a prompt injection from malicious @Docs
vulnerability.description: Cursor is a code editor built for programming with AI. In versions 0.45.0 through 0.48.6, the Cursor app introduced a regression affecting the set of file paths the Cursor Agent is permitted to modify automatically. Under specific conditions, the agent could be prompted, either directly by the user or via maliciously crafted context, to automatically write to files outside of the opened workspace. This behavior required deliberate prompting, making successful exploitation highly impractical in real-world scenarios. Furthermore, the edited file was still displayed in the UI as usual for user review, making it unlikely for the edit to go unnoticed by the user. This vulnerability is fixed in 0.48.7.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The vulnerability is a Prompt Injection issue in the Cursor code editor that affects the Cursor Agent, allowing it to write to unintended files. An attacker exploits this by tricking the Cursor Agent into automatically writing to files outside the intended workspace, typically by manipulating the app's context or user input, which requires specific deliberate prompting. This vulnerability could cause damage by allowing an attacker to modify sensitive files, potentially leading to data loss, corruption, or even unauthorized code execution.

vulnerability.title: Data exfiltration via AI plugin Jira tool
vulnerability.description: Mattermost versions 10.4.x <= 10.4.2, 10.5.x <= 10.5.0, 9.11.x <= 9.11.9 fail to restrict domains the LLM can request to contact upstream which allows an authenticated user to exfiltrate data from an arbitrary server accessible to the victim via performing a prompt injection in the AI plugin's Jira tool.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
Here's an explanation of the CVE vulnerability:
1. The vulnerability is a type of bug where attackers can trick the Mattermost AI plugin into contacting an arbitrary server, allowing them to access sensitive information. 2. The attacker exploits this vulnerability by sending a specially crafted prompt or message in the AI plugin's Jira tool, which tricks the plugin into making an unauthorized connection to the attacker's server. 3. This vulnerability could cause damage by allowing the attacker to exfiltrate sensitive data from an arbitrary server, which could include confidential business information, customer data, or even sensitive user credentials.

vulnerability.title: mannaandpoem OpenManus Prompt os command injection
vulnerability.description: A vulnerability classified as critical has been found in mannaandpoem OpenManus up to 2025.3.13. This affects an unknown part of the file app/tool/python_execute.py of the component Prompt Handler. The manipulation leads to os command injection. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The vulnerability CVE-2025-2733 is a Critical Prompt Injection flaw in a part of the OpenManus system, which allows an attacker to inject malicious commands. An attacker exploits this by crafting a malicious prompt that is executed by the system, potentially leading to unauthorized access or data tampering. This vulnerability can cause significant damage, allowing attackers to execute arbitrary system commands, potentially leading to data theft, system compromise, or even a full system takeover.

vulnerability.title: Kilo Code Prompt ClineProvider.ts ClineProvider injection
vulnerability.description: A vulnerability was detected in Kilo Code up to 4.86.0. Affected is the function ClineProvider of the file src/core/webview/ClineProvider.ts of the component Prompt Handler. Performing manipulation results in injection. The attack can be initiated remotely. The exploit is now public and may be used. Applying a patch is the recommended action to fix this issue.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
Here's a simplified explanation of the vulnerability:
