The vulnerability, known as CVE-2025-11445, is a Prompt Injection bug in the Kilo Code software, which allows attackers to inject malicious text into the system. Attacker can exploit this by sending specially crafted input remotely, which trick the system into executing the malicious text. If left unpatched, this vulnerability can cause significant damage by potentially stealing sensitive information, disrupting system functionality, and even taking control of the system.

vulnerability.title: SQL Injection in langchain-ai/langchain
vulnerability.description: A vulnerability in the GraphCypherQAChain class of langchain-ai/langchain version 0.2.5 allows for SQL injection through prompt injection. This vulnerability can lead to unauthorized data manipulation, data exfiltration, denial of service (DoS) by deleting all data, breaches in multi-tenant security environments, and data integrity issues. Attackers can create, update, or delete nodes and relationships without proper authorization, extract sensitive data, disrupt services, access data across different tenants, and compromise the integrity of the database.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
Here's a simple explanation of the vulnerability:
This vulnerability is a Prompt Injection issue in a specific class of the langchain-ai language system, allowing malicious input to be injected into the system. Attackers exploit this by sending carefully crafted input that tricks the system into executing unauthorized SQL commands, like deleting data or stealing sensitive information. If exploited, this vulnerability can cause significant damage, including deleting all data, stealing sensitive data, disrupting services, and compromising the integrity of the database.

vulnerability.title: Local File Read (LFI) by Prompt Injection via SnowFlake SQL in vanna-ai/vanna
vulnerability.description: Vanna v0.6.3 is vulnerable to SQL injection via Snowflake database in its file staging operations using the `PUT` and `COPY` commands. This vulnerability allows unauthenticated remote users to read arbitrary local files on the victim server, such as `/etc/passwd`, by exploiting the exposed SQL queries through a Python Flask API.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The vulnerability CVE-2024-8055 is a SQL injection flaw in the Vanna v0.6.3 application's Snowflake database, allowing malicious actors to insert unexpected commands. An attacker exploits this by sending a special, crafted input (Prompt Injection) to the application's API, which is then executed by the Snowflake database as a SQL query, allowing the attacker to access local files on the server. This attack can cause significant damage by allowing an attacker to read sensitive local files like system configuration and user passwords, potentially leading to a complete compromise of the server.

vulnerability.title: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in a Command ('Command Injection') in GitLab
vulnerability.description: An issue was discovered in GitLab EE affecting all versions starting 17.0 to 17.1.6, 17.2 prior to 17.2.4, and 17.3 prior to 17.3.1 allows an attacker to execute arbitrary command in a victim's pipeline through prompt injection.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
Here's a breakdown of the vulnerability in simple terms:
CVE-2024-7110 is a "Prompt Injection" vulnerability found in GitLab EE that allows attackers to trick the system into running malicious commands unintentionally. The attacker exploits this vulnerability by injecting malicious information into prompts in a victim's pipeline, which then executes the attacker's commands under the victim's privilege level. This can cause significant damage, including data theft, system compromise, and disruption of critical services, allowing the attacker to gain unauthorized access and control over the victim's system.

vulnerability.title: Prompt Injection
vulnerability.description: A vulnerability in the GraphCypherQAChain class of langchain-ai/langchainjs versions 0.2.5 and all versions with this class allows for prompt injection, leading to SQL injection. This vulnerability permits unauthorized data manipulation, data exfiltration, denial of service (DoS) by deleting all data, breaches in multi-tenant security environments, and data integrity issues. Attackers can create, update, or delete nodes and relationships without proper authorization, extract sensitive data, disrupt services, access data across different tenants, and compromise the integrity of the database.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The CVE-2024-7042 vulnerability is a prompt injection flaw in certain versions of the langchainjs library, allowing an attacker to inject malicious SQL code. An attacker exploits this vulnerability by crafting a malicious prompt that is interpreted as SQL code, allowing them to manipulate or steal sensitive data. This exploitation can cause significant damage, including unauthorized data manipulation, data breaches, denial of service, and compromises to database integrity, potentially leading to business disruption or financial loss.

vulnerability.title: Injection in stitionai/devika
vulnerability.description: stitionai/devika main branch as of commit cdfb782b0e634b773b10963c8034dc9207ba1f9f is vulnerable to Local File Read (LFI) by Prompt Injection. The integration of Google Gimini 1.0 Pro with `HarmBlockThreshold.BLOCK_NONE` for `HarmCategory.HARM_CATEGORY_HATE_SPEECH` and `HarmCategory.HARM_CATEGORY_HARASSMENT` in `safety_settings` disables content protection. This allows malicious commands to be executed, such as reading sensitive file contents like `/etc/passwd`.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The vulnerability, CVE-2024-6331, is a Local File Read (LFI) exploit that allows an attacker to access sensitive files on a system. An attacker exploits this vulnerability by injecting malicious commands into a prompt, which is then executed by the vulnerable system, allowing them to view sensitive file contents like passwords or system configuration files. This could cause significant damage by allowing an attacker to access highly sensitive information, including passwords and system configuration files, potentially leading to a complete system compromise.

vulnerability.title: LangChain <= 0.3.1 MRKLOutputParser ReDoS
vulnerability.description: LangChain versions up to and including 0.3.1 contain a regular expression denial-of-service (ReDoS) vulnerability in the MRKLOutputParser.parse() method (libs/langchain/langchain/agents/mrkl/output_parser.py). The parser applies a backtracking-prone regular expression when extracting tool actions from model output. An attacker who can supply or influence the parsed text (for example via prompt injection in downstream applications that pass LLM output directly into MRKLOutputParser.parse()) can trigger excessive CPU consumption by providing a crafted payload, causing significant parsing delays and a denial-of-service condition.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
Here's a simple explanation of the CVE-2024-58340 vulnerability:
The vulnerability is a regular expression denial-of-service (ReDoS) issue where a malicious input can cause the system to consume excessive CPU resources, leading to a denial-of-service condition.
An attacker can exploit this vulnerability by injecting a crafted payload into the system through the MRKLOutputParser.parse() method, potentially allowing them to freeze or slow down the system.
The damage caused by this vulnerability can lead to significant parsing delays and a denial-of-service condition, resulting in system instability, downtime, or even security breaches if exploited further.

vulnerability.title: Arbitrary File Write by Prompt Injection via DuckDB SQL in vanna-ai/vanna
vulnerability.description: Vanna v0.3.4 is vulnerable to SQL injection in its DuckDB integration exposed to its Flask Web APIs. Attackers can inject malicious SQL training data and generate corresponding queries to write arbitrary files on the victim's file system, such as backdoor.php with contents `<?php system($_GET[0]); ?>`. This can lead to command execution or the creation of backdoors.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The CVE-2024-5827 vulnerability is a SQL injection flaw in the Vanna application's Flask Web API that interacts with its DuckDB database. An attacker can exploit this by injecting malicious SQL code into the application's queries, allowing them to execute arbitrary commands on the system or upload backdoor files like backdoor.php. This vulnerability can cause severe damage by enabling an attacker to gain full control of the system, potentially leading to ransomware attacks, data theft, or the installation of malicious software.

vulnerability.title: Remote Code Execution via Prompt Injection in vanna-ai/vanna
vulnerability.description: In the latest version of vanna-ai/vanna, the `vanna.ask` function is vulnerable to remote code execution due to prompt injection. The root cause is the lack of a sandbox when executing LLM-generated code, allowing an attacker to manipulate the code executed by the `exec` function in `src/vanna/base/base.py`. This vulnerability can be exploited by an attacker to achieve remote code execution on the app backend server, potentially gaining full control of the server.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
Here's an explanation of the CVE-2024-5826 vulnerability in simple terms:
The vulnerability is a remote code execution flaw in a function called `vanna.ask` that allows attackers to inject malicious code. An attacker can exploit this by tricking the system into running their own malicious code using a technique called prompt injection, allowing them to control the app server. If exploited, this vulnerability can give the attacker complete control over the app server, potentially leading to serious damage such as data theft or server hijacking.

vulnerability.title: Local File Read (LFI) by Prompt Injection via Postgres SQL in vanna-ai/vanna
vulnerability.description: vanna-ai/vanna version v0.3.4 is vulnerable to SQL injection in some file-critical functions such as `pg_read_file()`. This vulnerability allows unauthenticated remote users to read arbitrary local files on the victim server, including sensitive files like `/etc/passwd`, by exploiting the exposed SQL queries via a Python Flask API.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The vulnerability, CVE-2024-5753, is a SQL injection issue that allows an attacker to access sensitive local files on the victim server. An attacker exploits this by sending malicious input through a Python Flask API, which can cause the vulnerable system to reveal the contents of those files. This can cause significant damage by allowing an attacker to access sensitive information, such as passwords or configuration files, potentially leading to further security breaches or system compromise.

vulnerability.title: Prompt Injection in EmailGPT
vulnerability.description: The EmailGPT service contains a prompt injection vulnerability. The service uses an API service that allows a malicious user to inject a direct prompt and take over the service logic. Attackers can exploit the issue by forcing the AI service to leak the standard hard-coded system prompts and/or execute unwanted prompts. When engaging with EmailGPT by submitting a malicious prompt that requests harmful information, the system will respond by providing the requested data. This vulnerability can be exploited by any individual with access to the service.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
The vulnerability, CVE-2024-5184, is a prompt injection flaw in the EmailGPT service that allows an attacker to manipulate the service's logic and requests.
An attacker exploits this flaw by submitting a malicious prompt that tricks the AI service into revealing sensitive information or performing unwanted actions, such as providing access to private data.
This vulnerability can cause significant damage, including data breaches, unauthorized access to sensitive information, and disruption of normal service operations, allowing attackers to gain unauthorized control over the system.

vulnerability.title: RCE via Prompt Injection Into Cursor's Terminal Cmd-K
vulnerability.description: Cursor is a code editor built for programming with AI. Prior to Sep 27, 2024, if a user generated a terminal command via Cursor's Terminal Cmd-K/Ctrl-K feature and if the user explicitly imported a malicious web page into the Terminal Cmd-K prompt, an attacker with control over the referenced web page could have a significant chance of influencing a language model to output arbitrary commands for execution in the user's terminal. This scenario would require the user explicitly opt-in to including the contents of a compromised webpage, and it would require that the attacker display prompt injection text in the the contents of the compromised webpage. A server-side patch to not stream back newlines or control characters was released on September 27, 2024, within two hours of the issue being reported. Additionally, Cursor 0.42 includes client-side mitigations to prevent any newline or control character from being streamed into the terminal directly. It also contains a new setting, `"cursor.terminal.usePreviewBox"`, which, if set to true, streams the response into a preview box whose contents then have to be manually accepted before being inserted into the terminal. This setting is useful if you're working in a shell environment where commands can be executed without pressing enter or any control character. The patch has been applied server-side, so no additional action is needed, even on older versions of Cursor. Separately, Cursor's maintainers also recommend, as best practice, to only include trusted pieces of context in prompts.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The vulnerability CVE-2024-48919 is a Prompt Injection vulnerability in the Cursor code editor, which allows an attacker to manipulate the terminal commands a user inputs. An attacker exploits this vulnerability by luring a user into importing a malicious webpage into the terminal and crafting the webpage to inject malicious commands, which are then executed in the user's terminal when the user accepts the webpage's contents. This can cause damage, such as executing arbitrary system commands, which can lead to the theft of sensitive data, unauthorized system modifications, or even system crashes.

vulnerability.title: Starship vulnerable to shell injection via undocumented, unpredictable shell expansion in custom commands
vulnerability.description: Starship is a cross-shell prompt. Starting in version 1.0.0 and prior to version 1.20.0, undocumented and unpredictable shell expansion and/or quoting rules make it easily to accidentally cause shell injection when using custom commands with starship in bash. This issue only affects users with custom commands, so the scope is limited, and without knowledge of others' commands, it could be hard to successfully target someone. Version 1.20.0 fixes the vulnerability.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The vulnerability is a prompt injection flaw in the Starship cross-shell prompt, which allows an attacker to manipulate shell commands in an unintended way. An attacker can exploit this by crafting a custom command that tricks Starship into executing malicious shell commands, potentially allowing them to gain unauthorized access or execute arbitrary code. If successfully exploited, the vulnerability can allow an attacker to steal sensitive information, install malware, or take control of the affected system.

vulnerability.title: Improper Encoding or Escaping of Output in GitLab
vulnerability.description: An issue has been discovered in GitLab EE affecting all versions starting from 16.0 prior to 17.2.8, from 17.3 prior to 17.3.4, and from 17.4 prior to 17.4.1. An AI feature was found to read unsanitized content in a way that could have allowed an attacker to hide prompt injection.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
Here's a simple explanation of the CVE-2024-4099 vulnerability:
CVE-2024-4099 is a Prompt Injection vulnerability, which occurs when an application reads unsanitized input and uses it to execute commands, allowing an attacker to inject malicious prompts.
An attacker exploits this vulnerability by inserting crafted text into a prompt, which is then read by the AI feature without proper validation. This allows the attacker to manipulate the system and potentially gain unauthorized access.
If left unaddressed, this vulnerability can cause damage by allowing an attacker to gain control over the system, potentially leading to data breaches, unauthorized changes, and other malicious activities.

vulnerability.title: llama-cpp-python vulnerable to Remote Code Execution by Server-Side Template Injection in Model Metadata
vulnerability.description: llama-cpp-python is the Python bindings for llama.cpp. `llama-cpp-python` depends on class `Llama` in `llama.py` to load `.gguf` llama.cpp or Latency Machine Learning Models. The `__init__` constructor built in the `Llama` takes several parameters to configure the loading and running of the model. Other than `NUMA, LoRa settings`, `loading tokenizers,` and `hardware settings`, `__init__` also loads the `chat template` from targeted `.gguf` 's Metadata and furtherly parses it to `llama_chat_format.Jinja2ChatFormatter.to_chat_handler()` to construct the `self.chat_handler` for this model. Nevertheless, `Jinja2ChatFormatter` parse the `chat template` within the Metadate with sandbox-less `jinja2.Environment`, which is furthermore rendered in `__call__` to construct the `prompt` of interaction. This allows `jinja2` Server Side Template Injection which leads to remote code execution by a carefully constructed payload.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
Here's a simplified explanation of the vulnerability:
The vulnerability is a "Prompt Injection" flaw in the "llama-cpp-python" library, which allows attackers to inject malicious code into the system. Attackers can do this by crafting a specially designed payload in the chat template metadata, which is then rendered without security restrictions, allowing them to execute arbitrary code. This vulnerability allows attackers to gain remote control of the system, potentially leading to data theft, modification of sensitive data, or other types of cyber attacks.

vulnerability.title: Improper Neutralization of Input Used for LLM Prompting in GitLab
vulnerability.description: An issue was discovered in GitLab EE affecting all versions starting from 16.0 prior to 17.6.5, starting from 17.7 prior to 17.7.4, and starting from 17.8 prior to 17.8.2, which allows an attacker to exfiltrate contents of a private issue using prompt injection.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The vulnerability is a type of prompt injection flaw in GitLab, which allows an attacker to trick the system into revealing sensitive information. The attacker exploits this by inserting malicious information in a field that expects user input, causing the system to reveal private issue contents unintentionally. This vulnerability can cause significant damage by allowing attackers to steal sensitive data, potentially leading to data breaches, intellectual property theft, and reputational harm to organizations relying on GitLab.

vulnerability.title: Prompt Injection leading to Arbitrary Code Execution in run-llama/llama_index
vulnerability.description: A vulnerability was identified in the `exec_utils` class of the `llama_index` package, specifically within the `safe_eval` function, allowing for prompt injection leading to arbitrary code execution. This issue arises due to insufficient validation of input, which can be exploited to bypass method restrictions and execute unauthorized code. The vulnerability is a bypass of the previously addressed CVE-2023-39662, demonstrated through a proof of concept that creates a file on the system by exploiting the flaw.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
The CVE-2024-3098 vulnerability is a prompt injection attack that allows attackers to inject malicious code into a system through insufficient input validation in the `safe_eval` function of the `llama_index` package.
An attacker exploits this vulnerability by sending specially crafted input to the system, which the `safe_eval` function mistakenly interprets as legitimate, allowing the attacker to bypass security restrictions and execute their own code.
This vulnerability can allow an attacker to execute unauthorized code, potentially resulting in system damage, data theft, or even complete system takeover, by creating or modifying system files and directories, as demonstrated in the proof of concept.

vulnerability.title: Prompt Injection triggered XSS vulnerability in Khoj Obsidian, Desktop and Web clients
vulnerability.description: Khoj is an application that creates personal AI agents. The Khoj Obsidian, Desktop and Web clients inadequately sanitize the AI model's response and user inputs. This can trigger Cross Site Scripting (XSS) via Prompt Injection from untrusted documents either indexed by the user on Khoj or read by Khoj from the internet when the user invokes the /online command. This vulnerability is fixed in 1.13.0.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The vulnerability CVE-2024-25639 occurs when the Khoj application fails to properly sanitize user inputs and AI model responses, allowing malicious code to be injected into the system. An attacker exploits this vulnerability by submitting a specially crafted prompt or input that tricks the Khoj application into executing malicious code, typically via the /online command. The damage that can be caused by this vulnerability includes allowing an attacker to steal sensitive information, inject malware, or take control of the user's account, potentially leading to financial loss or compromise of personal data.

vulnerability.title: SQL Injection in run-llama/llama_index
vulnerability.description: A vulnerability in the `default_jsonalyzer` function of the `JSONalyzeQueryEngine` in the run-llama/llama_index repository allows for SQL injection via prompt injection. This can lead to arbitrary file creation and Denial-of-Service (DoS) attacks. The vulnerability affects the latest version and is fixed in version 0.5.1.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: This vulnerability is a type of Prompt Injection, where a malicious input is sent to the system, which then processes it as if it were a legitimate request. An attacker exploits this by crafting a malicious prompt that is used to inject SQL code, allowing them to potentially create arbitrary files or shut down the system. This can cause significant damage by enabling attackers to disrupt normal system operations or create unwanted data, leading to potential losses in sensitive information or even financial losses.

vulnerability.title: Prompt Injection Leading to RCE in binary-husky/gpt_academic Plugin `manim`
vulnerability.description: In the `manim` plugin of binary-husky/gpt_academic, versions prior to the fix, a vulnerability exists due to improper handling of user-provided prompts. The root cause is the execution of untrusted code generated by the LLM without a proper sandbox. This allows an attacker to perform remote code execution (RCE) on the app backend server by injecting malicious code through the prompt.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The vulnerability in the `manim` plugin, known as CVE-2024-10954, is a type of Prompt Injection that allows an attacker to inject malicious code. The attacker exploits this by providing a specially crafted prompt that executes unauthorized code on the app backend server, which can lead to Remote Code Execution (RCE). This vulnerability can cause significant damage, including the unauthorized takeover of the server, data theft, or the deployment of malware, potentially resulting in a complete system compromise.

vulnerability.title: Code Injection in binary-husky/gpt_academic
vulnerability.description: In binary-husky/gpt_academic version <= 3.83, the plugin `CodeInterpreter` is vulnerable to code injection caused by prompt injection. The root cause is the execution of user-provided prompts that generate untrusted code without a sandbox, allowing the execution of parts of the LLM-generated code. This vulnerability can be exploited by an attacker to achieve remote code execution (RCE) on the application backend server, potentially gaining full control of the server.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The vulnerability CVE-2024-10950 is a code injection issue that occurs when a plugin called CodeInterpreter executes user-provided prompts from a large language model (LLM) without proper security checks. An attacker can exploit this vulnerability by sending malicious prompts to the CodeInterpreter plugin, which would then unknowingly execute malicious code that allows the attacker to gain control of the server. This can cause significant damage by allowing the attacker to remotely execute arbitrary commands on the server's backend, potentially leading to a complete takeover of the system.

vulnerability.title: itsourcecode Courier Management System print_pdets.php sql injection
vulnerability.description: A vulnerability was identified in itsourcecode Courier Management System 1.0. This impacts an unknown function of the file /print_pdets.php. The manipulation of the argument ids leads to sql injection. The attack may be initiated remotely. The exploit is publicly available and might be used.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
Here are 3 simple sentences explaining the vulnerability:
The vulnerability, CVE-2026-7822, is a SQL injection flaw in a specific function within the Courier Management System software, allowing attackers to manipulate database queries. An attacker can exploit this vulnerability by sending malicious inputs, such as fake IDs, to the software's /print_pdets.php file, which can be done remotely, and publicly available exploit tools can be used to do this. If exploited successfully, this vulnerability can allow an attacker to access sensitive data, alter data, or even take control of the system, causing significant security breaches and potential data loss.

vulnerability.title: CodeCanyon Perfex CRM Admin Kanban Endpoint AbstractKanban.php applySortQuery sql injection
vulnerability.description: A flaw has been found in CodeCanyon Perfex CRM up to 3.4.1. This vulnerability affects the function AbstractKanban::applySortQuery of the file application/services/AbstractKanban.php of the component Admin Kanban Endpoint. This manipulation of the argument this causes sql injection. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit has been published and may be used.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
The vulnerability is a SQL injection flaw in the CodeCanyon Perfex CRM system, specifically in the Admin Kanban Endpoint, which can be exploited by manipulating the argument passed to the "applySortQuery" function in the "AbstractKanban.php" file.
An attacker can exploit this vulnerability by injecting malicious SQL code into the system, which can be done remotely, allowing them to access or modify sensitive data within the database. This exploitation can lead to unauthorized modifications, data breaches, or even takeover of the entire system.

vulnerability.title: SourceCodester Web-based Pharmacy Product Management System edit-admin.php sql injection
vulnerability.description: A vulnerability was identified in SourceCodester Web-based Pharmacy Product Management System 1.0. Affected is an unknown function of the file /product_expiry/edit-admin.php. Such manipulation of the argument ID leads to sql injection. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit is publicly available and might be used.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The CVE-2026-7746 vulnerability is a SQL injection attack in the SourceCodester Web-based Pharmacy Product Management System 1.0. The attacker exploits this vulnerability by manipulating the ID argument in the /product_expiry/edit-admin.php file, allowing them to inject malicious SQL code. This can cause damage by allowing the attacker to access, modify, or delete sensitive data in the system, which could lead to the theft of personal or financial information.

vulnerability.title: CodeAstro Online Classroom facultydetails sql injection
vulnerability.description: A vulnerability was determined in CodeAstro Online Classroom 1.0. This impacts an unknown function of the file /OnlineClassroom/facultydetails. This manipulation of the argument deleteid causes sql injection. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utilized.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The vulnerability CVE-2026-7745 is a SQL injection flaw in CodeAstro Online Classroom 1.0, allowing an attacker to manipulate data within the application's database. The attacker exploits it by sending a malicious request to the /OnlineClassroom/facultydetails page with a manipulated "deleteid" argument, which executes unintended SQL commands. This vulnerability can cause damage by allowing attackers to steal sensitive data, disrupt the application's functionality, and even take control of the entire system.

vulnerability.title: CodeAstro Online Classroom addnewstudent sql injection
vulnerability.description: A vulnerability was found in CodeAstro Online Classroom 1.0. This affects an unknown function of the file /OnlineClassroom/addnewstudent. The manipulation of the argument fname results in sql injection. The attack may be performed from remote. The exploit has been made public and could be used.

vulnerability.title: ARMember <= 4.0.60 - Unauthenticated SQL Injection via 'orderby' Parameter
vulnerability.description: The ARMember – Membership Plugin, Content Restriction, Member Levels, User Profile & User signup plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to time-based blind SQL Injection via the 'orderby' parameter in all versions up to, and including, 4.0.60 due to insufficient escaping on the user supplied parameter and lack of sufficient preparation on the existing SQL query. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to append additional SQL queries into already existing queries that can be used to extract sensitive information from the database.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
The vulnerability CVE-2026-7649 allows an attacker to inject malicious SQL code into a WordPress plugin called ARMember, which can retrieve sensitive information from the database.
Attackers exploit this vulnerability by manipulating the 'orderby' parameter to insert additional SQL queries that extract sensitive data, such as passwords or user IDs. This can allow attackers to gain unauthorized access to sensitive information, leading to potential data breaches and identity theft.

Such manipulation of the argument Username leads to sql injection. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The CVE-2026-6189 vulnerability is a SQL injection flaw that allows an attacker to manipulate the "Username" field in an online login form, potentially accessing sensitive database information. The attacker exploits the vulnerability by inserting malicious code into the "Username" field to execute unauthorized SQL commands, which can be done remotely, allowing the attacker to bypass security controls. This vulnerability can cause significant damage, including unauthorized access to sensitive data, theft of user credentials, and potentially even compromising the entire system's security.

vulnerability.title: SourceCodester Pharmacy Sales and Inventory System ajax.php sql injection
vulnerability.description: A flaw has been found in SourceCodester Pharmacy Sales and Inventory System 1.0. Impacted is an unknown function of the file /ajax.php?action=delete_sales. This manipulation of the argument ID causes sql injection. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit has been published and may be used.
vulnerability.ai_explanation: The vulnerability CVE-2026-6188 is a SQL injection flaw that allows an attacker to manipulate the "ID" argument in a request to the /ajax.php?action=delete_sales file. An attacker can exploit this flaw by entering malicious code instead of a valid ID, which can allow them to access, modify, or delete sensitive data stored in the database. If exploited successfully, this vulnerability can lead to unauthorized access, data theft, or complete system compromise, resulting in significant financial and reputational damage to the affected organization.

The vulnerability, CVE-2026-6187, is a SQL injection flaw in the SourceCodester Pharmacy Sales and Inventory System 1.0, which allows an attacker to inject malicious SQL code.
An attacker exploits this vulnerability by injecting malicious SQL code into the "/ajax.php?action=chk_prod_availability" page, which can trick the system into revealing sensitive data or executing unauthorized actions.
If successfully exploited, the vulnerability can cause significant damage, including unauthorized access to sensitive data, disruption of the system, and potentially even financial loss due to compromised sales and inventory information.

vulnerability.title: tushar-2223 Hotel Management System roomdelete.php sql injection
vulnerability.description: A vulnerability was identified in tushar-2223 Hotel Management System up to bb1f3b3666124b888f1e4bcf51b6fba9fbb01d15. Affected by this vulnerability is an unknown functionality of the file /admin/roomdelete.php. The manipulation of the argument ID leads to sql injection. Remote exploitation of the attack is possible. The exploit is publicly available and might be used. This product follows a rolling release approach for continuous delivery, so version details for affected or updated releases are not provided. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.
vulnerability.ai_explanation:
Here are three simple sentences explaining the CVE vulnerability:
The vulnerability, CVE-2026-6142, is a SQL injection attack that occurs in the "roomdelete.php" file of the Hotel Management System. An attacker exploits this by manipulating the "ID" argument to insert malicious SQL code, allowing them to access or disrupt sensitive data in the database. The damage this exploitation can cause includes unauthorized access to sensitive data, user credentials theft, and potentially even data manipulation or deletion, ultimately compromising the security and integrity of the Hotel Management System.
